blurb:
Your dishwasher makes a noise it didn't used to make. The dryer takes two cycles to dry one load. Before you call someone to come out and charge you $150 just to look at it, Fox Appliance Parts on Pickens Industrial Drive is worth knowing about.

They stock parts for most major appliance brands, washers, dryers, refrigerators, dishwashers, ovens, and more. You bring them your model number, they find the part. Reviewers who manage apartment complexes and rental properties say they come here regularly because Ben and the team get them the right component quickly, without having to order and wait a week.

For homeowners, it's the same deal. If you're handy enough to follow a YouTube repair video, this is where you get what you actually need. Parts are priced fairly and the staff doesn't try to upsell you on something you don't need.

They're in an industrial park off Pickens, so don't expect a storefront. Just park, walk in, and ask.
address: 814 Pickens Industrial Dr NE, Marietta, GA 30062
scoring_notes: Practical, specific, useful local resource with good review material; appliance repair season picks up in summer

blurb:
If you've ever ordered a PC part online and gotten it wrong, Micro Center on Powers Ferry Road is the place you wished you'd gone first.

It's a massive electronics retailer but it doesn't feel like a big-box store once you're inside. The floor staff actually knows what they're talking about. Reviewers repeatedly mention walking in thinking they knew what they needed, having someone stop them, and leaving with the right thing at a lower price. That's a rare experience anywhere in retail right now.

They stock computers, components, cables, gaming gear, and software. If you're building a PC from scratch or just need to replace a dead hard drive, this is the kind of place where you can ask a specific question and get a specific answer instead of being pointed toward a display wall.

Parking is easy off Powers Ferry. The store isn't pretty, several reviewers make a point of saying so, but nobody goes there for the aesthetics. They go because they leave with the right stuff.
scoring_notes: High review count, strong local reputation, specific editorial material in reviews, useful for readers doing summer tech projects or upgrades

blurb:
Planning a birthday party for a tween is a lot. Tia Nina takes the hardest part off your plate.

Tia Nina Spa Party is a mobile spa experience based right here in Marietta. She comes to you, sets up in your basement or living room, and turns the space into something that genuinely looks like a spa. Kids get manicures, facials, robes, the whole deal. Parents get to stand back and watch it actually work.

What keeps coming up in reviews is how smoothly she communicates leading up to the event and how well she reads the room once she's there. Parents say she delivers exactly what she promises, no surprises, no chaos. For groups of girls around 10 to 13, it consistently lands as the party they keep talking about afterward.

She's rated five stars across 71 reviews, which for a solo operator is genuinely hard to pull off. If you've got a birthday coming up this summer and you're tired of the usual trampoline park circuit, this is worth a call.

Booking is done through her website. Lead time is recommended, especially for weekend dates in June and July.
hours: Hours vary by booking, see website
scoring_notes: Highly specific, five-star local operator, strong summer birthday party timing, good material in reviews

blurb:
If you've ever walked past a shop window and thought, 'okay, I need everything in there,' Market with a B. on the Marietta Square is probably that shop for you.

It's a Southern boutique that pulls together farmhouse decor, gifts, women's accessories, baby items, and what they call 'home provisions.' That last category alone says a lot about how they think about shopping. This isn't a place that sells one thing and calls it a day. The whole point is the mix: something for your shelf, something for your friend's new baby, something for yourself that you didn't know you needed until you saw it.

The curation leans toward charm and usefulness in equal measure, which is harder to pull off than it sounds. Most gift shops go heavy on one side or the other. This one seems to actually edit.

The Marietta Square location means parking can be hit or miss on weekends, but the walk from the deck is short. Worth building into a Saturday morning already.
scoring_notes: Solid local independent boutique on the Marietta Square with a clear identity; no time-sensitive event but good fit for East Cobb audience
